2605.07727.title: Drifting Field Policy: A One-Step Generative Policy via Wasserstein Gradient Flow
2605.07727.paper_summary: We propose Drifting Field Policy (DFP), a non-ODE one-step generative policy built on the drifting model paradigm. We frame the policy update as a reverse-KL Wasserstein-2 gradient flow toward a soft target policy, so that each DFP update corresponds to a gradient step in probability space. By construction, this gradient is decomposed into an ascent toward higher action-value regions and a score matching with the anchor policy as a trust region. We further derive a simple, tractable surrogate of the otherwise intractable update loss, akin to behavior cloning on top-K critic-selected actions. We find empirically that this mechanism uniquely benefits the drifting backbone owing to its non-ODE parameterization. With one-step inference, DFP achieves state-of-the-art performance on several manipulation tasks across Robomimic and OGBench, outperforming ODE-based policies.
2605.07727.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了漂移場政策（DFP），這是一種基於漂移模型範式的非ODE單步生成政策。<lb/>我們將政策更新框架設置為朝向軟目標政策的反KL Wasserstein-2梯度流，這樣每次DFP更新對應於概率空間中的一個梯度步驟。<lb/>根據構造，這個梯度被分解為朝向更高行動價值區域的上升以及與錨點政策的分數匹配作為信任區域。<lb/>我們進一步推導出一個簡單且可處理的代理，來替代原本難以處理的更新損失，類似於在頂級批評者選擇的行動上進行行為克隆。<lb/>我們實證發現，這一機制因其非ODE參數化而獨特地使漂移骨幹受益。<lb/>通過單步推理，DFP在Robomimic和OGBench的幾個操作任務上達到了最先進的性能，超越了基於ODE的政策。
2605.07727.author: Juil Koo et.al.
2605.07727.authors: Juil Koo, Mingue Park, Jiwon Choi, Yunhong Min, Minhyuk Sung
